questions.text: The classical dance form 'Sattriya', introduced by the great Vaishnava saint Mahapurush Sankaradeva, originated in which Indian state?
Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the appointment, oath, and conditions of service of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India?
Who among the following was the celebrated court poet of King Harshavardhana and the author of his biography, 'Harshacharita'?
Which of the following is the highest mountain peak in Peninsular India and the Western Ghats?
The concept and methodology of estimating the 'Poverty Line' in India was extensively reviewed and revised by which of the following expert committees in 2009?
The deficiency of which of the following vitamins is the primary cause of the neurological and cardiovascular disease known as 'Beriberi'?
The bestselling autobiography titled 'Playing It My Way' is authored by which famous Indian sports personality?
The concept of 'Fundamental Duties' enshrined in Part IVA of the Indian Constitution was adopted from the constitution of which nation?
The First Battle of Panipat, which decisively ended the Delhi Sultanate and marked the beginning of the Mughal Empire in India, was fought in which year?
The Keibul Lamjao National Park, globally recognized as the world's only floating national park, is located on the Loktak Lake in which state?
In the field of optics, what is the SI unit used to express the optical Power of a Lens?
Who among the following holds the distinction of being the first Indian athlete to win an individual gold medal at the Olympic Games?
In India, which central institution operates under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation to calculate and publish the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) data?
The socio-religious reform movement known as 'Brahmo Samaj' was founded by whom in 1828 in Calcutta?
Which internationally recognized boundary line demarcates the border between the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh and the Tibet region of China?
According to Article 157 of the Indian Constitution, what is the minimum age prescribed for a person to be eligible for appointment as the Governor of a State?
What is the correct chemical formula for Chloroform, a dense liquid historically used as an anesthetic in medicine?
The vibrant and sacred Buddhist festival known as 'Saga Dawa', which celebrates the birth, enlightenment, and parinirvana of Lord Buddha, is predominantly observed in which state?
Part IX of the Indian Constitution, officially added by the landmark 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992, deals exclusively with which of the following?
The city of Thanjavur (Tanjore), renowned for the magnificent Brihadisvara Temple, served as the primary imperial capital of which ancient South Indian dynasty?
Which pea-sized gland located at the base of the human brain is often referred to as the 'Master Gland' because it controls the functions of several other endocrine glands?
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI), the central banking institution of the country, was formally established on April 1 of which year?
The Atal Tunnel, recognized as one of the longest highway tunnels in the world constructed above 10,000 feet, runs under which strategic mountain pass?
Who was the historic first recipient of India's highest sporting honor, the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award (now renamed Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award) in 1991-92?
In December 1916, the historic political agreement known as the 'Lucknow Pact' was signed between the Indian National Congress and which other major organization?
options: B. The All India Muslim League
